entries.promptText: Instrumental loop for a festival strategy game. Mood: relaxed daytime crowd energy, warm and optimistic, medium-low intensity. Style: indie electronic with soft percussion, clean bass, subtle guitar plucks. Constraints: no vocals, seamless loop behavior, avoid harsh transients.

Instrumental loop for a festival routing game. Mood: momentum and urgency for mid-game rounds, bright and driving. Style: punchy dance-pop groove, syncopated percussion, energetic synth stabs. Constraints: no vocals, seamless loop, keep melodic hooks short and repeatable.

Instrumental loop for final high-pressure rounds in a festival game. Mood: intense but triumphant, high energy, late-night main-stage vibe. Style: electro-rock hybrid with strong kick, sidechained synths, big risers. Constraints: no vocals, seamless loop, avoid muddy low-end.
Short game UI stinger for artist spawn. Bright electronic pluck with tiny crowd texture tail. Length around one second.
Soft swoosh sound for drawing path lines on touch screen. Smooth airy texture, light friction feel, no click at end. Length around one second.
Positive festival delivery cue. Short crowd cheer with a clean upward musical hit. Length around 1.3 seconds.
Bigger combo success cue for chaining deliveries. Layered crowd shout plus sparkling synth flourish. Celebratory and punchy, around two seconds.
Negative feedback cue for missed artist. Gentle descending tone with subtle crowd disappointment texture. Should feel informative, not harsh; around 1.5 seconds.
Short playful chatter cue for two artists colliding into conversation. Light babble-like texture with warm tone, non-verbal. Length under one second.
Cue for distraction trigger (fans/food/merch pull). Curious attention-grabbing tone with light sparkle. Length around 1.2 seconds.
Level completion fanfare for festival game. Triumphant but compact orchestral-electronic hit with crowd uplift. Length around 2.5 seconds.
Level failed sting for festival game. Sympathetic descending motif, soft impact, emotional but not punitive. Length around two seconds.
Low-time warning pulse for gameplay urgency. Short ticking pulse with subtle high-frequency alert. Length about one second and loop-friendly cadence.
